assets.abstract: Empathy is a core human capacity that underpins social life. Utilizing hyperscanning electroencephalography (EEG), we tested how empathy to others' distress synchronizes brains without social cues. Mothers and adolescents (study 1, n = 100) underwent empathy-to-distress paradigm in separate rooms. Event-related interbrain connectivity was computed in four 500 ms time-windows following exposure to empathy-to-distress and control stimuli. Interbrain synchrony of fronto-temporal regions emerged thr

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Neural synchrony — interpersonal brain-to-brain coupling, hyperscanning, joint music-making, collective entrainment.
